bids.reasoning: At the start of the negotiation, I will aim for a bid with maximum utility according to my preferences since there's no time pressure yet. My most preferred values across all issues are "valueC" due to their highest utilities. I will propose this to see if the opponent is willing to match my ideal bid or counter with their own preferences.

At this early stage of the negotiation (t=0.02), my focus is on maximizing my utility. The opponent has opened with a bid that provides me the least utility across all issues, suggesting they may be anchoring with a low offer. My previous offer was aimed at maximizing my utility with all top-preference values. Since there is minimal time pressure, I will maintain a firm stance, proposing the same high-utility offer as before. This will signal to the opponent the necessity of moving towards a more balanced offer.

We are near the beginning of the negotiation process, so I aim to stick closely to my preferred utility. I observe the opponent's offers have consistently low utility for me, indicating they are likely holding a firm position without concessions. Their messages suggest a willingness to continue negotiations, but I will continue with a high-utility offer to make sure solid ground is established early on. Time pressure is minimal at this stage of negotiation, so I maintain a strong position by proposing high-utility value options for all issues.

Given the very early stage of negotiation (t=0.06), my strategy is to continue advocating for my top utility values. The opponent's offers have been consistently low in utility for me, as they propose valueA across all issues, which is my least preferred. Their messages suggest they are framing their offer as fair, but their consistent low-utility offers signal they are holding firm on their initial stance. I will make a small concession on a less important issue (issueA), switching from valueC to valueB, to show some flexibility while maintaining high utility on crucial issues like issueF.

I aim to maximize my utility by initially focusing on maintaining high-value selections for the most important issues, particularly issueF, while making some concessions in less significant issues like issueA and issueB where lower-value choices have been consistent in the opponent's offers. As time is still early (t=0.08), I prioritize my highest utility options to push the result in my favor but have started introducing a lower value for issueA to signal willingness to move from my initial stance. I aimed to maximize my utility by prioritizing the most important issues like issueF, while also recognizing the need for some concessions as the opponent has not been moving much from their initial position.
